sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I notice something important! The charcoal square moved! It was at [44,50]-[49,53] before, and now it's at [44,46]-[49,49]. It moved LEFT by 4 columns. Also the bottom yellow strip moved from [44,54]-[49,55] to [44,50]-[49,51], also left by 4 columns.
So clicking objects can cause other objects to move! Let me reconsider the game mechanics.
Let me also note the click results:
1: white bg → +1 (total 1)
2: top yellow → +2 (total 3)
3: top blue → +1 (total 4)
4: charcoal → +1 (total 5)
5: left black → +1 (total 6)
6: bottom yellow → +2 (total 8)
7: bottom blue → +1 (total 9)
Now I have 9 charcoal pixels. The right black bar is the only object I haven't clicked. Let me click it.
But wait, I also noticed the objects moved. The charcoal square and bottom yellow strip both moved left by 4. This is interesting - maybe clicking the bottom blue square caused these objects to shift?
Let me click the right black bar now. It's at [28,40]-[31,63], center at row 29, col 51.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# IMPORTANT: Objects moved! Charcoal square went from [44,50]-[49,53] to [44,46]-[49,49] (left 4)
# Bottom yellow strip went from [44,54]-[49,55] to [44,50]-[49,51] (left 4)
# This happened after clicking bottom blue square at (33,61)
#
# Charcoal pixels: 8 → 9 (added 1)
# Level not complete yet.
#
# Remaining unclicked object: right black bar [28,40]-[31,63]
# Click center: row 29, col 51

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 51}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
